Exp 5: Cross-Model 5-Predictor RSA

Generated: 2026-03-31 07:52

11 models across verb, object, and period token positions. 5-predictor regression (A-E) on conditions C1-C4 with 10K permutations and FDR correction.

Model families:

  • LLaMA: LLaMA-2-13B Chat, LLaMA-2-13B Base, LLaMA-3-8B Instruct, LLaMA-3-8B Base
  • Gemma: Gemma-2-2B-IT, Gemma-2-2B Base, Gemma-2-9B-IT, Gemma-2-9B Base
  • Qwen: Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct, Qwen-2.5-7B Base, Qwen3-8B
Significance: * pFDR < .05, ** pFDR < .01, *** pFDR < .001. Filled dots on plots = FDR-significant layers.
5-Predictor Model Specification:
A (Full Attribution): both sentences in C1 (mental_state) — tests bound {subject + mental verb + object}
B (Mental Verb + Object): both in C1 or C2 — tests mental verb with object, no subject required
C (Mental Verb Presence): both in C1, C2, or C3 — tests presence of mental verb in any form
D (Verb + Object): both in C1, C2, or C4 — tests verb-object binding regardless of verb type
E (Subject + Verb + Object): both in C1 or C4 — tests full sentence structure regardless of verb type

Key question: Does predictor A (full mental state attribution) capture unique variance beyond what is explained by its component parts (B-E)?

Contents

  • 1. Model A Peak Summary (All Models)
  • 2. Model A — Cross-Model by Position
  • 3. All Predictors — Family-Grouped Grid
  • 4. Per-Model Detailed Tables

1. Model A (Full Attribution) — Peak Summary

Peak statistics for predictor A at each token position across all 11 models. Model A captures the unique variance attributable to bound mental state attribution ({subject + mental verb + object}) after controlling for component features.

2. Model A — Cross-Model by Position

Each panel shows one model (family-grouped). Lines show Model A beta across layers at verb (green), object (orange), and period (blue) positions. Filled dots = FDR-significant. Does the attribution signal emerge at the same position and depth across architectures?

3. All Predictors — Family-Grouped Grid

Each panel shows one model with all 5 predictors (A-E) at the period position. Family-grouped layout: LLaMA (row 1), Gemma (row 2), Qwen (row 3).

4. Per-Model Detailed Tables

Full predictor summary and emergence narrative for each model.
